record.text:
「平和国家」の根拠は憲法9条だと認めた高市首相。
当たり前のようなことですが、実はこの間政府は「国連憲章を遵守する平和国家」などと憲法を、とりわけ9条を巧妙に取り除き、意味を変えてきました。

9条が日本の平和国家を基礎づけていると認めるなら、その9条を変えることは平和国家でなくなるという宣言にほかなりません。

変えるべきは9条ではなく、9条を認識しながら9条を壊してきた政治の方です。

SpaceXの上場のための届出書S-1には、注記として投資家向けのリスク開示があり、そのひとつが「Grokが性的画像の生成拡散ツールとして一部の政府からBanされること」。

いやいや、リスクだと分かっているなら、対処しろよ！　と思うわけですが、社会との摩擦に対して、なにがなんでも対処しないのがイーロン・マスクの信念のようにも観察されます。

社会との摩擦を気にかけず、規制にも服さないテック企業をどう扱うか、これは世界の課題です。

ほか、S-1には「宇宙データセンターが商業的に成立しないリスク」や「火星植民ができないリスク」なども記載。これらは訴訟対策と思われます。

Prins Bernhard (56) is de tweede zoon van prinses Margriet en Pieter van Vollenhoven. Het is nog onzeker of hij dit jaar aanwezig is op Koningsdag, want in januari werd bekend dat hij en zijn vrouw Annette (54) gaan scheiden na ruim 25 jaar huwelijk.

Bernhard is zelfstandig ondernemer. Hij bezit vastgoed in Amsterdam – hij wordt de ‘pandjesprins’ genoemd – en is medeaandeelhouder van het circuit in Zandvoort. Zijn ondernemersgeest liet hij al zien tijdens zijn studie economie aan de Rijksuniversiteit Groningen. Met een paar vrienden richtte hij Ritzen Koeriers op, vernoemd naar de toenmalig Onderwijsminister Jo Ritzen. Het bedrijf liet goedkoop pakjes bezorgen door studenten die met hun OV-jaarkaart gratis konden reizen.

Bernhard is ook oprichter van Stichting Lymph&Co die zich inzet voor onderzoek naar lymfeklierkanker, een ziekte die hij zelf ook heeft gehad.

Chatbot Copilot raadt mij ten strengste af hier op voorhand over te schrijven. De veiligheid van het staatshoofd valt onder de nationale veiligheid, lees ik.

De code is bovendien om de koning zijn rust te gunnen. Begrijpelijk. En terwijl ik me inbeeld hoe de Rijksvoorlichtingsdienst mij ’s nachts uit bed klopt, val ik in slaap.
Uitchecken

In een poging toch wat op te vangen, meld ik me ’s ochtends bij de ontbijttafel. Dat voor het hotel een bijzondere dag aanbreekt, is nog steeds amper te merken.

De badkamer van nummer 11.
De badkamer van nummer 11. Foto: LC

In plaats van boter, smeer ik per ongeluk smeerkaas onder mijn kaas. Ik eet het toch op. Dat zou voor de koning lastiger zijn. Als iemand dat zou zien, zou dat nog jaren stof kunnen geven voor anekdotes.

Bij het uitchecken om 11 uur probeer ik het toch maar. „Zit de spanning er al een beetje in, nu de koning komt?”, vraag ik voorzichtig zodat niemand het hoort.

De vrouw achter de balie draait er vakkundig omheen. „We merken er nog niet veel van. Maar met de koning in Dokkum wordt het vast gezellig!”

According to Sex Matters, organisations - including private ones - who *want* to be trans inclusive can't be, because of the Equality Act. 

I've seen some very sketchy interpretations of the EA as of late, but this one is the most bizarre, I think.

Sex Matters are demanding biological sex on Digital ID. Below are suggestions for when one should have to use their ID to "verify" their sex.

For the avoidance of doubt – this is a call for the effective repeal of the Gender Recognition Act, in breach of our human rights obligations & EU Law.

images.alt:
Examples include:

joining a dating service
joining a single-sex association, such as Girlguiding or the Women’s Institute
applying for a bursary, prize or award that is only for women 
joining a gym and using its ID to access male or female changing rooms
registering for sport with a national governing body or sports team, or for competitions
applying for a job in social care, policing, prisons or a single-sex service
seeking to rent in a shared house, homestay, hall of residence or dormitory
registering with a healthcare or social-care provider

images.alt:
✅ Fact‑check: “There were other events at the hotel, so they secured just the portion for the WHCD. It obviously was enough security, because the shooter never got close to Trump.”
1. TRUE — The Washington Hilton did have multiple events happening
News outlets covering the April 25, 2026 incident reported that:

The Washington Hilton is a multi‑event venue with several ballrooms and meeting spaces.

The Secret Service secured only the areas required for the White House Correspondents’ Dinner (WHCD).

This is standard protocol: they do not lock down an entire hotel unless the President is staying overnight.

So yes — only the WHCD perimeter was hardened.

2. TRUE — The shooter was outside the secure perimeter
According to reporting from NBC News, CBS News, AP, and others:

The gunfire occurred outside the hotel, in a publicly accessible area.

The suspect never breached the magnetometer‑screened zone where Trump and attendees were located.

Secret Service agents evacuated Trump immediately, following standard procedure.

So the statement “the shooter never got close to Trump” is accurate based on current reporting.

3. TRUE — People inside the ballroom were never in direct physical danger
Based on all available information:

The shooter was not inside the building.

The shooter was not within line‑of‑sight of the President.

The shooter was apprehended outside after an exchange of gunfire with law enforcement.

So the security perimeter did its job.

images.alt: „It's no coincidence that Kenya became home to what would ultimately turn into one of the most exploitative forms of labor that went into the creation of ChatGPT. Kenya was among the top destinations that Silicon Valley had been outsourcing its dirtiest work to for years. With the many other countries that the tech industry relegates to this role, Kenya shares a common denominator: It is poor, in the Global South, with a government hungry for foreign investment from richer countries. All of these are a part of Kenya's legacy of colonialism, which has left it without well-developed institutions to protect its citizens from exploitation and often in the throes of economic crisis, both of which make circumstances ripe for overseas companies to find an immiserated pool of labor that will do piecework under almost any conditions.“

record.text:
Is AI Cannibalizing Human Intelligence? A Neuroscientist's Way to Stop It

Vivienne Ming critiques the AI industry's focus on providing answers without considering how it impacts human cognitive development. Her experiment explored the performance of AI, human, and hyb…

Telegram AI Digest
#ai #news

external.description: Vivienne Ming critiques the AI industry's focus on providing answers without considering how it impacts human cognitive development. Her experiment explored the performance of AI, human, and hybrid teams in predicting real-world events. Human teams struggled, while large AI models performed better but not as well as prediction markets. Hybrid teams showed varied results, with most failing by simply adopting AI's answers. However, a small percentage of hybrid teams excelled by using AI as a sparring partner, questioning its assumptions and challenging its conclusions. These teams, through critical engagement with AI, rivaled or surpassed the prediction market's accuracy, demonstrating the power of human-AI collaboration. The author argues that current AI systems often prioritize ease of access over fostering critical thinking, which is detrimental. This leads to the "Information-Exploration Paradox," where readily available information hinders human exploration and understanding. Ming's new book emphasizes the importance of building human capacity in the age of AI. The book suggests using AI to explore uncertainty and challenging its answers before accepting them. She advocates for new performance benchmarks to assess the effectiveness of human-AI hybrid teams.
external.title: Is AI Cannibalizing Human Intelligence? A Neuroscientist's Way to Stop It

images.alt: „It's no coincidence that Kenya became home to what would ultimately turn into one of the most exploitative forms of labor that went into the creation of ChatGPT. Kenya was among the top destinations that Silicon Valley had been outsourcing its dirtiest work to for years. With the many other countries that the tech industry relegates to this role, Kenya shares a common denominator: It is poor, in the Global South, with a government hungry for foreign investment from richer countries. All of these are a part of Kenya's legacy of colonialism, which has left it without well-developed institutions to protect its citizens from exploitation and often in the throes of economic crisis, both of which make circumstances ripe for overseas companies to find an immiserated pool of labor that will do piecework under almost any conditions.“

images.alt:
Der Manga »Nito no Joreishi« von Hiromi Ichikawa ist am 12. März 2026 im »Weekly Young Jump« Magazin von Shueisha zu Ende gegangen. Die Reihe lief in Japan von Oktober 2024 bis März 2026 und umfasst aktuell 5 Bände. Der sechste und zugleich finale Band soll am 16. Mai 2026 erscheinen. Eine englische Veröffentlichung der einzelnen Kapitel erscheint bei Manga Plus, während die Reihe hierzulande bislang nicht auf Deutsch erhältlich ist.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Gotsuji und Uruka ziehen als Exorzisten von Auftrag zu Auftrag und stellen sich Tag für Tag gefährlichen Geistern. Doch hinter ihren Einsätzen steckt mehr als reine Pflicht: Gotsuji jagt einen bestimmten bösen Geist, der einst eine Frau tötete, die ihm viel bedeutete. Während ihn Trauer und Rache antreiben, bleibt Uruka an seiner Seite, auch wenn sie seine Besessenheit nicht vollständig versteht.

images.alt:
Der Manga »Shin Tennis no Ojisama« von Takeshi Konomi befindet sich auf der Zielgeraden. Nachdem am 21. März 2026 von nur noch 5 verbleibenden Veröffentlichungen bis zum Finale die Rede war, rückt das Ende der Reihe immer näher. Der Manga läuft seit März 2009 im »Jump SQ.«-Magazin von Shueisha und umfasst aktuell 46 Bände. Band 47 erscheint voraussichtlich am 4. Juni 2026. Eine englische Veröffentlichung gibt es bislang nicht.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Nach den erbitterten Kämpfen der nationalen Mittelschulmeisterschaft erhalten 50 der stärksten Spieler eine Einladung ins japanische U17-Trainingscamp. Auch Ryoma Echizen und seine Teamkameraden von der Seishun Academy stellen sich dieser neuen Herausforderung. Doch im Camp warten Gegner, die stärker, härter und erfahrener sind als alles, was sie bisher erlebt haben.

🧠 Forget amyloid — dopamine may be the real key to Alzheimer's.

Tohoku × UC Irvine found dopamine drops to under 1/5 normal in Alzheimer's mice. Levodopa, a 50-yr-old Parkinson's drug, restored memory. Nature Neuroscience just shook the field.
